mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signals.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 2% signals robust demand conditions, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive GDP signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2% signals inflationary pressures, prompting central bank rate hikes that compress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative CPI signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.5% combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for combined INDPRO/HOUST signals.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer savings (RSAFS) below $340 billion signals weakened consumer confidence, reducing demand for goods/services and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative RSAFS signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 115 signals robust manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
